copy.text: An evolving exhibition of woodgrain, light, and the quiet geometry of the handmade.

Central nave
An evolving exhibition of woodgrain, light, and the quiet geometry of the handmade.

We collect work that listens to its room. From the late twentieth century to the present day, the holdings favour pieces shaped by material, scale and place — art that stands differently here than anywhere else.

Elin Marsh
Elin Marsh, curator since 2011, on hanging work in the timber hall. Every placement is tested against the light at three hours of the day before it stays.

rationale: Standard-tier layout-pattern showcase: the measured opening bookend, then every contracts/layout-patterns/ pattern derived deterministically from its contentShape; non-drawable archetypes (row/grid/bento/band/overlay) degrade to the generic-flow safety net pending the harvest/contract work.
